verdict: One delivery idiom (Key emit/observe), but the state riding it is not one language, and many of the richest cross-scale edges are declared-not-delivered. Consequence computes then dies at two structural seams — emitter-side (strategic Keys leave sub-scale targets[] unpopulated) and consumer-side (registry names consumers whose own consumes lists omit the type) — and finally at the terminal renderer, which subscribes to * yet fires no trigger for the flagship collisions (Thread, battle_concluded). The Key pipe is uniform; the fluid is not. doc:null/[ASSUMPTION] debt clusters on exactly the connective modules (domain_actions, engine_clock, npc_memory) the collision engine depends on, so emergent narrative rides declarations, not implementations.
key_points: Flattened all dossier edges[] into declared-vs-implemented: DIVERGENT (declared consumer doesn't consume), DECLARED-ONLY (emitter never populates targets[]), IMPLEMENTED (mostly bottom-up + intra-provincial faction<->settlement).

CONFIRMED module_contracts.yaml L844 gap_note A4: scene.combat_resolved 'declared with consuming_systems [npc_behavior, faction_layer, articulation]... those modules' contract consumes lists do not yet name them' — flagship consequence-dies-in-transit.

CONFIRMED scale_transitions_v30 §12.4 L331-332: 8 down-seams / 15 type-edges whose consume intent is registry-canonical but whose strategic emitters leave targets[] sparse — top-down consequence delivers blind.
CONFIRMED domain_actions doc:null, consumes:[] (module_contracts L470-475): the player's primary strategic verb is the least-specified module and the source of 3 down-seams.
CONFIRMED articulation consumes '*' (L768) emits [] (L769): renderer RECEIVES thread/combat Keys via wildcard but its 10-trigger ruleset fires no cut scene for them — terminal sink death.

Q-smooth corpus verdict: uniform at transport layer, NOT at vocabulary layer — MS/RS dual-naming, Turmoil vs Political-Stability, 3 enum-fork Key types, self-contradicting 2-cycle contract, faction_politics doc:null emitting shared Keys.

doc:null/[ASSUMPTION] debt is not evenly-distributed: it clusters on connective tissue (domain_actions, engine_clock, npc_memory) — the two modules most load-bearing for cross-scale consequence are the least authored.
Two structural death-points generalize the individual edge flags into a class: emitter-side sparse-targeting and consumer-side contract omission are the same defect in two directions.

findings.claim: scene.combat_resolved is the flagship consequence-dies-in-transit edge: an entire physics-rich subsystem's only output Key is both declared-but-unconsumed (its named consumers' contract consumes lists omit it) AND health-bar-shaped (scene_id/outcome/casualties/wounds, no scar/identity/stake), so personal combat's consequence escapes to nothing.

The player's primary strategic verb resolves at the faction-stat ledger and never reaches sub-scale: domain_actions is doc:null with consumes:[], and §12.4 confirms it is the emitter for 3 of the 8 registry-canonical down-seams whose targets[] are unpopulated — top-down consequence delivers blind for the headline choice.

The P-14 spine's richest cross-scale collision generator has BOTH its highest-narrative-value sinks structurally absent — combat_engine_v1 has zero thread interface (ED-911) and articulation renders zero thread events despite receiving them via wildcard — so Thread collisions compute across faction/settlement but cannot reach the player.
findings.cite: references/module_contracts.yaml L767-769 (articulation consumes */emits []); threadwork dossier + articulation dossier §3.1 trigger table; ED-911

findings.claim: The individual divergent/declared-only edges are one defect class in two directions: emitter-side sparse targets[] (down-seams) and consumer-side contract-consumes omission (scene.combat_resolved) both mean a registry-canonical edge delivers to nothing; naming this as a class shows the corpus lacks any gate that a declared consumer actually consumes.

State does not cross boundaries in one idiom at the vocabulary layer even though it does at transport: same world-track dual-named MS/RS, same victory-track Turmoil vs Political-Stability, 3 Key types with permanence/time_horizon outside the substrate §2.2 enum, and a 2-cycle whose two contract blocks disagree on resolution — a corpus-wide Q-smooth failure no shape-layer instrument gates.
findings.cite: params/threadwork.md L136-138 vs threadwork_v30 §5; canon/02_canon_constraints.md §B GD-1; key_type_registry_v30 L933/977/997 vs key_substrate_v30 §2.2; module_contracts ~L153 vs ~L442

findings.claim: The doc:null/[ASSUMPTION] debt is not evenly distributed debt but concentrates on the connective modules the collision engine most depends on (domain_actions, engine_clock, npc_memory), so the emergent-narrative inputs are authored while the wiring that would carry them cross-scale is [ASSUMPTION]-grade — the North-Star tax is inert state plus untraceable consequence, not merely unfinished specs.

The meta-layer a session bootstraps the interdependency picture from is stale exactly where the corpus is most alive: mechanics_index marks mass_battle/social_contest not-built while both are mature, and no sim ratification round-trips back — a meta-cohesion break that makes every downstream edge-status claim unverifiable from the registries alone.
findings.cite: canon/mechanics_index.yaml L468-491 vs tests/coverage_matrix.md; references/canonical_sources.yaml L156-161
